front: Poem: 'A poem should not mean / But be'
Poem: the title object should be 'palpable and mute / as a globed fruit' and 'motionless in time / As the moon climbs'
Poem: the title object should be 'silent as the sleeve-worn stone / Of casement ledges' and 'Dumb / as old medallions to the thumb'
Play: Nickles and Mr. Zuss put on masks representing Satan and God and bet on how the title banker will react when they ruin his life, in a retelling of Job
Play: the title character's children Jonathan and Mary die in a car crash, and an explosion destroys his bank; Eliphaz, Bildad, and Zophar appear as comforters
Poem: describes night sweeping across Ecbatan, Kermanshah, Baghdad, Palmyra, Lebanon, Crete, and Spain; addressed to a figure 'face down beneath the sun'
Author: wrote 'Voyage to the Moon' commissioned by the New York Times for the Apollo 11 front page
Author: appointed Librarian of Congress by Franklin Roosevelt; judged Yale Series of Younger Poets between Benet and Auden
Play: a woman rises from the dead to prophesy 'masterless men will take a master'; the Conqueror is an empty suit of armor; a Radio Announcer notes 'people invent their oppressors'
back: The Fall of the City (Archibald MacLeish)

front: Poem: describes Vasserot the armless ambidextrian 'lighting / A match between his great and second toe' and Teeny 'about to cough / In waltz-time'
